templates.text: Silence, engineered. The stone here was cut to absorb sound, to create a space where the only noise is internal.
You sat with what you know. In stillness, the gaps in your knowledge became visible in a way combat never allows.
The floor bears the impression of many seekers who came before you, cross-legged, breathing, waiting for clarity that may or may not arrive.
The chamber offered you a choice between ease and depth. The dungeon has filed your answer under character.
Incense ash in a stone bowl. Someone maintained this room — tended it, kept it prepared — for someone exactly like you.
The chamber does not speak. It facilitates speaking to yourself, which the dungeon considers a more honest form of dialogue.
What you emerged with — whether insight or merely rest — the chamber provided it. That is what meditation chambers are for.
The door out feels heavier than the door in. Not because anything was added. Because something was examined.
The dungeon approves of seekers who stop. Most do not stop. The ones who do tend to go further.
